hook: “Mini-trampolines for your feet” remembered from 90s kids’ TV
fate: The Nickelodeon-associated 1990s version faded from mainstream toy shelves by the late 1990s or early 2000s. Later Moon Shoes-style products continued under other manufacturers and online retail listings.
summary: Nickelodeon Moon Shoes were the 1990s plastic-and-bungee version of the strap-on bounce toy often billed as “mini-trampolines for your feet.” The broader moon-shoe concept dates back earlier, but the version remembered by 90s kids used hard plastic frames and elastic bands to create a springy, moon-walk-like bounce. The Nickelodeon-associated version became a memorable fad toy, then faded from mainstream shelves as the decade ended. Later non-Nickelodeon Moon Shoes-style products continued to appear, so the most accurate status is that the original Nickelodeon-era version is discontinued while later versions exist.

keywords: what happened to Nickelodeon Moon Shoes
Moon Shoes mini trampolines for your feet

overview_md:
**Nickelodeon Moon Shoes** were the 1990s plastic-and-bungee version of the strap-on bounce toy often advertised as *“mini-trampolines for your feet.”* Kids stepped into chunky plastic frames suspended by elastic bands, creating a springy bounce meant to feel like walking on the moon.

The broader idea of moon shoes goes back much earlier, including metal versions from the 1950s, but the version most 90s kids remember was tied to Nickelodeon-era toy marketing. That specific Nickelodeon-associated version faded from mainstream shelves by the late 1990s or early 2000s.

Moon Shoes did not disappear completely as a concept. Later non-Nickelodeon Moon Shoes-style products continued to show up through other manufacturers and online retail listings. So the clearest way to describe them is: the original Nickelodeon-era version is discontinued, while later Moon Shoes-style products still exist.

timeline.text: Earlier metal moon shoes exist; these are distinct from the later Nickelodeon plastic/bungee version.
The Nickelodeon-associated plastic/bungee Moon Shoes era begins; the toy is marketed as 'mini-trampolines for your feet'.
Moon Shoes become a recognizable 90s kids' TV-era fad toy.
The original Nickelodeon-associated retail era fades from mainstream toy shelves.
Later Moon Shoes-style products continue to appear through other manufacturers and online retail listings.
